description:
Roll-up epic for the blockers that make `mix hex.publish` refuse to run (or produce a legally unusable / docs-less package). Source: cold-context ship-readiness assessment (2026-06-22), packaging grade D, docs grade D+.

Until every child here lands, the package CANNOT be published. The library internals are strong (tests B+, ADRs solid); this epic is purely the publishable-envelope mechanics.

## Verified findings
- `mix.exs` has NO `description:` — hex hard-refuses without it.
- NO `LICENSE` file and no `licenses:` key — hex requires a declared license; without the file the package is legally unusable.
- NO `package:` block at all (licenses/links/maintainers).
- NO `ex_doc` dep and no `docs:` config — hexdocs page would be empty despite excellent moduledoc coverage (57/57 modules, all public fns have @spec).

## Done = `mix hex.publish --dry-run` is green.

## Children
- B1: description + package metadata (licenses, links, maintainers)
- B2: LICENSE file
- B3: ex_doc dep + docs config
- B4: dry-run capstone — verify publish + confirm GitHub repo/tag exist

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATION SUMMARY (paused on capstone)

Children landed on epic/y8o (3 of 4 closed):
- mutagen-y8o.2 LICENSE + licenses key — closed (c7f4a01)
- mutagen-y8o.1 description + package metadata — closed (f42a4ca)
- mutagen-y8o.3 ex_doc dep + docs config — closed (918bc37)

Capstone mutagen-y8o.4 — OPEN/ESCALATED. Metadata half PASSES (`mix hex.build` clean, no missing-metadata refusals; all hard blockers resolved). Remaining blockers are human ship-checkpoints the orchestrator does not perform:
  1. Create public GitHub repo autom8things/mutagen_ex + remote + push epic/y8o + v0.1.0 tag/release.
  2. hex auth + real `mix hex.publish`.

Bounces: 0. Scope expansions: 0. User escalations: 1 (capstone human actions).
Epic stays in_progress; not closing (that's /deliver's job post-publish).

title: [EPIC] hex.pm publish — hard blockers
